titleStmt.title: Letter from Henry Lawson to Reginald H. Brade, 11 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Henry Lawson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Major General Henry Lawson (1859-1933) to Reginald Brade (1864-1933) confirming Patrick Alphonsus Carroll's arrival at the Curragh on 9th February 1916.Patrick Alphonsus Carroll was a veterinary surgeon based in Carlow town who had qualified from the Royal College of Veterinary Surgeons. The Army Veterinary Corps was well tested throughout the Great War. There were about 2.5 million admissions mainly on the Western Front and 80% of injured animals were treated and returned to duty. Major Rainey would survive the war and later document the story of the AVC in the Great War in 1925.Sir Reginald Brade served as the Under-Secretary of State for War from 1914 to 1920.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Head Quarters, Irish Command, Parkgate, Dublin, 11th February, 1916. V.S./568. Sir, With reference to your No. 1045/16 (V.D.),
dated 19th January, 1916, I have the honour to report
that Mr P.A. Carroll, M.R.C.V.S., arrived at the
Curragh on the 9th February, 1916, and reported
himself to the Officer Commanding, Station Veterinary
Hospital there, for duty, in anticipation of being
gazetted to a temporary commission as Lieutenant in
the Army Veterinary Corps. I have the honour to be,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, Henry Lawson Major
for Major-General,
Commanding the Troops in Ireland.
The Secretary, War Office, London, S.W.
